stemHtml: Rain is formed within warm clouds when a particle, such as a grain of dust, attracts a drop of moisture to itself. In a next step, this initial cloud droplet will..

options.a: be taken higher by updrafts in the cloud, thereby freezing and growing in size due to collisions with other ice crystals.
options.b: collide with other droplets, thereby growing in size and eventually forming a rain drop.
options.c: merge with other droplets, a process which is greatly improved if the colliding drops feature the same electrical charge.
options.d: leave the cloud, eventually forming the final rain drop at and below the cloud base, as this is where the humidity is highest.
